title: Claire Chase

infobox.value: (born 1978) is a solo flutist, collaborative artist, curator and advocate for new and experimental music.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Chase has won the [Avery Fisher Prize](/wiki/Avery_Fisher_Prize "Avery Fisher Prize"), which recognizes musical excellence, vision, and leadership. In 2012, Chase was awarded a [MacArthur Fellowship](/wiki/MacArthur_Fellowship "MacArthur Fellowship")—the so-called "genius" award.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American musician

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This biographical article **is written [like a résumé](/wiki/R%C3%A9sum%C3%A9 "Résumé")**. Please [help improve it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Claire_Chase&action=edit) by revising it to be [neutral](/wiki/Wikipedia:NPOV "Wikipedia:NPOV") and [encyclopedic](/wiki/Wikipedia:MOSBIO "Wikipedia:MOSBIO"). *(April 2023)* |

| Claire Chase | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | 1978 (age 47–48)  [Leucadia, California](/wiki/Leucadia,_California "Leucadia, California"), U.S. |
| Genres | Classical |
| Instrument | Flute |

Musical artist

**Claire Chase** (born 1978) is a solo flutist, collaborative artist, curator and advocate for new and experimental music.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Chase has won the [Avery Fisher Prize](/wiki/Avery_Fisher_Prize "Avery Fisher Prize"), which recognizes musical excellence, vision, and leadership. In 2012, Chase was awarded a [MacArthur Fellowship](/wiki/MacArthur_Fellowship "MacArthur Fellowship")—the so-called "genius" award.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Claire_Chase&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Chase was born in 1978 and grew up in [Leucadia, California](/wiki/Leucadia,_California "Leucadia, California"). She made her solo debut with the [San Diego Symphony](/wiki/San_Diego_Symphony "San Diego Symphony") at age 14 in 1992.

While attending [Oberlin College](/wiki/Oberlin_College "Oberlin College"), where she studied with [Michel Debost](/wiki/Michel_Debost "Michel Debost"), she received the [Theodore Presser](/wiki/Theodore_Presser "Theodore Presser") Foundation Award in 1999 which she used to commission new compositions for the flute.[[2]](#cite_note-oberlin-2) She received her [B.M.](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Music "Bachelor of Music") from Oberlin in 2001.[[3]](#cite_note-concertartists-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Claire_Chase&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

After graduating from Oberlin, Chase founded the [International Contemporary Ensemble](/wiki/International_Contemporary_Ensemble "International Contemporary Ensemble") (ICE) in 2001, and was its Executive/Artistic Director until 2017.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

After winning first prize in the [Concert Artists Guild](/wiki/Concert_Artists_Guild "Concert Artists Guild") competition in 2008, she made her [Carnegie Hall](/wiki/Carnegie_Hall "Carnegie Hall") debut in 2010 at the Weill Recital Hall.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Chase has premiered over 100 new solo works for the flute that incorporate extended techniques and electro-acoustic elements.[[3]](#cite_note-concertartists-3) Her first solo album, *Aliento* was released in 2009 and was one of [*Time Out Chicago*](/wiki/Time_Out_(magazine) "Time Out (magazine)")'s Top 10 Classical Albums of 2009.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Chase has performed world-wide as a soloist and chamber musician in venues including [(Le) Poisson Rouge](/wiki/(Le)_Poisson_Rouge "(Le) Poisson Rouge"), [Miller Theatre](/wiki/Miller_Theatre "Miller Theatre"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) and [Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts](/wiki/Lincoln_Center_for_the_Performing_Arts "Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts") in New York City, the [John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts](/wiki/John_F._Kennedy_Center_for_the_Performing_Arts "John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts") in Washington D.C., the [Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum](/wiki/Isabella_Stewart_Gardner_Museum "Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum") in Boston, the [Sibelius Academy](/wiki/Sibelius_Academy "Sibelius Academy") in Helsinki, the [Palacio de Bellas Artes](/wiki/Palacio_de_Bellas_Artes "Palacio de Bellas Artes") in Mexico City.[[3]](#cite_note-concertartists-3)

Over the past decade, Claire Chase has premiered hundreds of new works for the flute in performances throughout the Americas, Europe, and Asia. She continues to champion new music throughout the world by building organizations, forming alliances, pioneering commissioning initiatives, and supporting educational programs that reach new audiences.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

She began "Density 2036" in 2014, a 22-year project to commission a significant body of new music for the flute, culminating in the one-hundredth anniversary of [Edgard Varèse](/wiki/Edgard_Var%C3%A8se "Edgard Varèse")'s 1936 "[Density 21.5](/wiki/Density_21.5 "Density 21.5")".[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In the fall of 2017 Chase was appointed Professor of the Practice in the Music Department at [Harvard University](/wiki/Harvard_University "Harvard University").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Chase held the 2022–23 Richard and Barbara Debs Composer's Chair at [Carnegie Hall](/wiki/Carnegie_Hall "Carnegie Hall").[[12]](#cite_note-Carnegie_Hall-12)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Claire_Chase&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Discography")]

* *Density 2036: parts v 2017-2018* (2020, Corbett vs. Dempsey Records)[[13]](#cite_note-:1-13)
* *Density 2036: parts iv 2016* (2020, Corbett vs. Dempsey Records)[[13]](#cite_note-:1-13)
* *Density 2036: parts iii 2015* (2020, Corbett vs. Dempsey Records)[[13]](#cite_note-:1-13)
* *Density 2036: parts i & ii 2013-2104* (2020, Corbett vs. Dempsey Records)[[13]](#cite_note-:1-13)
* *Density* (2013, New Focus Recordings)[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* *Terrestre* (2012, New Focus Recordings)[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* *Died in the Wool* (2011, [Samadhi Sound](/wiki/Samadhi_Sound "Samadhi Sound"))
* *Bright and Hollow Sky* (2011, New Focus Recordings)
* *Undersong* (2011, [Mode Records](/wiki/Mode_Records "Mode Records"))
* *Aliento* (2009, New Focus Recordings)
* *Enter Houses Of* (2009, [Tzadik Records](/wiki/Tzadik_Records "Tzadik Records"))
* *Complete Crumb Edition, Vol. 12* (2008, [Bridge Records](/wiki/Bridge_Records,_Inc. "Bridge Records, Inc."))

**With [John Zorn](/wiki/John_Zorn "John Zorn")**

* *[On the Torment of Saints, the Casting of Spells and the Evocation of Spirits](/wiki/On_the_Torment_of_Saints,_the_Casting_of_Spells_and_the_Evocation_of_Spirits "On the Torment of Saints, the Casting of Spells and the Evocation of Spirits")* (Tzadik, 2013)
* *[Fragmentations, Prayers and Interjections](/wiki/Fragmentations,_Prayers_and_Interjections "Fragmentations, Prayers and Interjections")* (Tzadik, 2014)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Claire_Chase&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Awards")]

* 2017 [Avery Fisher Prize](/wiki/Avery_Fisher_Prize "Avery Fisher Prize")[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* 2012 [MacArthur Fellowship](/wiki/MacArthur_Fellows_Program "MacArthur Fellows Program")[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* 2010 [Carlos Surinach](/wiki/Carlos_Surinach "Carlos Surinach") Prize[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* 2008 First Prize [Concert Artists Guild](/wiki/Concert_Artists_Guild "Concert Artists Guild") Competition[[3]](#cite_note-concertartists-3)
* 2001 First Prize National Young Artist Competition[[3]](#cite_note-concertartists-3)
* 1999 [Theodore Presser](/wiki/Theodore_Presser_Company "Theodore Presser Company") Foundation Award[[2]](#cite_note-oberlin-2)
* 1996 [Presidential Scholar Award](/wiki/Presidential_Scholars_Program "Presidential Scholars Program") from the [National Foundation for Advancement in the Arts](/wiki/YoungArts "YoungArts")[[20]](#cite_note-iceorg-20)
* 1995 First Prize California Young Artists Competition[[20]](#cite_note-iceorg-20)
